title: Repeater behavior
description: Configure behavior = REPEATER for change-triggered looping and rejection reloop semantics without cron or poller scheduling.
openingSummary: REPEATER is the WorkstationKind scheduling behavior for change-triggered loops. Set behavior to REPEATER when a step should reloop on upstream change or rejection routing, without cron schedules or long-lived poller ingress.

whatItCovers.title: What It Covers
whatItCovers.body: Repeater behavior is the Factory WorkstationKind for change-triggered looping and rejection reloop semantics in you-agent-factory. This page covers the behavior = REPEATER discriminator, reloop and routing semantics, the validated behavior overlay embed, a minimal valid configuration, an incompatible misuse of cron, operational cautions, and compatible WorkstationType companions. Use it when a workstation must re-enter after change or rejection rather than fire once under STANDARD.

keyConcepts.title: Key Concepts
keyConcepts.body: The discriminator field is behavior with value REPEATER. REPEATER schedules a workstation when inputs become ready, then reloops when upstream places change or when rejection routing returns work to the loop. Behavior is independent of type: REPEATER pairs with every published WorkstationType, including AGENT_RUN and POLLER_RUN. REPEATER is not CRON — it excludes the cron field — and it is not STANDARD fire-once or POLLER long-lived scheduling.

howToUse.title: How To Use
howToUse.body: Author a workstation with behavior REPEATER. Keep type on the runtime axis for the execution family you need. Bind a worker when the type requires one. Wire inputs plus outputs, onContinue, onRejection, and onFailure so rejection and continue routes can re-enter the loop. Do not set cron on REPEATER. Choose STANDARD for fire-once dispatch, CRON for schedules, or POLLER for long-lived poller ingress.

variantFields.title: Variant Fields
variantFields.body: The embed below shows the live Workstation base definition with REPEATER overlay applicability. REPEATER has no selected exclusive fields. Excluded fields belong to other WorkstationType families or to CRON scheduling and must not appear on this behavior. Open the full Factory schema reference for exhaustive field lookup rather than copying contract text into this page.

examples.title: Examples
examples.body: Use the minimal valid example when starting a REPEATER workstation. The misuse example shows cron on REPEATER — cron belongs on behavior CRON and is excluded from REPEATER.

operationalCautions.title: Operational Cautions
operationalCautions.body: REPEATER steps fail closed when reloop never terminates, when rejection routes omit onRejection, when concurrency limits block re-entry, or when route fields leave an outcome unmapped. Do not use cron schedules or long-lived poller ingress under REPEATER — those belong on CRON and POLLER. Wire onRejection deliberately so rejection reloops are intentional, not accidental infinite retries.

limitsAndAssumptions.title: Limits And Assumptions
limitsAndAssumptions.body: This page is the direct /docs/workstations/repeater surface for REPEATER scheduling. It is not a sync of packaged CLI docs, not a WorkstationType guide, and not the STANDARD, CRON, or POLLER behavior contracts. Compatible WorkstationType companions name the runtime pairing for this behavior; they do not redefine REPEATER fields. Worker backends stay on the workers family.

links.companionIntro: REPEATER is compatible with every published WorkstationType. Bind a matching Worker for worker-backed types; LOGICAL_MOVE stays worker-free.
links.misuseNote: cron is a CRON scheduling field. REPEATER excludes it. Use behavior CRON when the step must run on a schedule.
links.failureReloopCause: Change or continue routes keep re-entering without a stop condition — tighten onContinue/onRejection or add a guard
links.failureRejectionCause: Rejection outcome has no onRejection route — wire rejection places so reloops are intentional
links.failureConcurrencyCause: Workstation or resource limits blocked a reloop dispatch — raise limits or reduce parallel work
links.failureRoutingCause: Outcome has no authored route — wire outputs, onContinue, onRejection, or onFailure
links.failureCronMisuseCause: cron declared under REPEATER — move the schedule to behavior CRON
